headline: Notre Dame's Carr takes down Georgia in offensive shootout
body: CJ Carr orchestrated a clinic in the passing game, completing 28 of 42 attempts for 412 yards and three touchdowns to lead Notre Dame past Georgia 41-28 in a Week 3 matchup that showcased two capable offensive systems unable to finish the same way. Georgia's Giles Gilbert kept pace with his own three touchdown passes and 271 yards through the air, but the Notre Dame quarterback's superior efficiency and command of the rhythm offense made the difference when it mattered. The gap in total yardage, 551 to 361, tells a story of Notre Dame's ability to sustain drives where Georgia could not.

Georgia's defense entered the contest ranked among the stingier units in the league, but Notre Dame's balance kept the unit honest all afternoon. Smothers carried the ball 16 times for 80 yards, not flashy but steady enough to make third downs convertible and keep the Georgia pass rush from settling in. Meanwhile, Carr had time to find receivers like McPherson, who made five receptions for 99 yards despite drawing attention downfield. Georgia's Mondon Jr. recorded four tackles and a sack, his best effort insufficient to shift momentum when it counted.

The turnover battle revealed another layer: Notre Dame coughed up one fumble but recovered the fumble with it, erasing its one mistake. Georgia committed nothing but also generated nothing in the explosive moments that swing close games. Young's six receptions and 108 yards kept the passing chains alive for Georgia, yet the volume required to stay competitive worked against clock management and field position. By the fourth quarter, Notre Dame had the breathing room Giles and his offense never quite found.
turningPoint: Notre Dame's sustained drive in the third quarter, where Carr moved the ball methodically without taking negative plays, pushed the lead to double digits while Georgia's defense grew tentative.

unsungHero.name: Smothers
unsungHero.why: Sixteen carries might not leap off the stat sheet, but grinding out 80 yards kept Georgia's front seven honest and allowed Carr to operate in rhythm without needing hero throws on every snap.
